title: Roku Q1 2023 Earnings Report
text: Roku posted Q1 2023 revenue of $741 million, representing 1% year-over-year growth and surpassing analyst expectations of $708.49 million. Net loss was $193.6 million, or $1.38 per share, slightly better than anticipated $1.37 loss. Platform gained 1.6 million active accounts, exceeding Wall Street's consensus of 1.14 million. Total active accounts reached 71.6 million. Users streamed 25.1 billion hours, representing 20% year-over-year growth, translating to 3.9 streaming hours per account daily, a record high. Platform segment revenue declined 1% to $635 million. Macro environment remained challenged with pressured spending in financial services and media/entertainment sectors offset by improvements in travel and wellness. Operating costs surged 42% to $550 million. For Q2, management projected $770 million in revenue. Company expects macro uncertainties to persist throughout 2023.
